hero.heading: Your first visit,
hero.subhead: Everything you need to know before you arrive. We made this part easy on purpose.

newPatient.heading: What to bring on
newPatient.note: Your first appointment is longer than you might expect — we want to understand your full health picture, not just check a box.

checklist: Relevant medical records or lab results
Completed new patient forms (sent before your visit)

visitFlow.heading: Modern tools,
visitFlow.intro: We use modern systems quietly in the background so your visit can be about your health — not paperwork. Here's what to expect:

steps.title: Before your visit
steps.body: Complete registration, history, and insurance on your own device — usually a few minutes the night before. Copays are handled up front so check-in at the front desk is quick.

steps.title: In the room
steps.body: Your doctor walks in already familiar with what you submitted. The time in the room is for your health, not for catching up on forms.

steps.title: After your visit
steps.body: A short text-message survey gives you a moment to tell us if anything could have gone better. We read every response.

steps.title: Between visits
steps.body: Refills, school or work forms, quick questions, and care coordination are all handled by secure text or a phone call to our staff. Whichever you prefer — both reach the same team.

insurance.heading: Most plans
insurance.note: Don't see your plan? Call (772) 569-3212 and we'll verify your coverage.

selfPay.label: Self-Pay Welcome
selfPay.note: A simple, transparent option — common for patients with high-deductible plans, those without insurance, or anyone who prefers to pay directly. Call our office for current rates.

faqs.a: Yes — our inaugural panel is open and intentionally limited. Book online or call us at (772) 569-3212 to claim your place.
We accept most major insurance plans including Medicare, Blue Cross Blue Shield, Aetna, Cigna, United Healthcare, and others. Call our office to verify your specific plan before your first visit.

faqs.q: How do I transfer from Vero Beach Pediatrics?
faqs.a: Transferring is simple — we already have a relationship with the VBP team. Call our office and let us know you're a VBP family. We'll coordinate your records and make the transition as seamless as possible.

faqs.q: What happens if I'm sick and need to be seen quickly?
faqs.a: Call or message us in the morning. We make every effort to accommodate same-day visits for urgent concerns. When our schedule is full, virtual care provides a prompt alternative so you're not left waiting.

faqs.q: What should I bring to my first appointment?
faqs.a: Please bring a valid photo ID, your insurance card, a list of current medications and dosages, any relevant medical records or lab results, and a list of questions or concerns you'd like to discuss.
Yes, telehealth visits are available for appropriate follow-ups and consultations. Contact our office to determine if your visit qualifies for a virtual appointment.

cta.heading: Ready when you are.
cta.subhead: Our founding panel is forming now. Book your first visit online or give us a call.

hero.heading: Get started in
hero.subhead: Complete your registration online before your first visit. The form is encrypted and sent securely to our care team.

newPatientRegistration.reassurance: Your information is protected. This form uses HIPAA-compliant encryption and is only accessible to our care team. We never share your data outside of your care.

fallback.heading: Registration form coming soon.
fallback.body: Our online registration is being finalized. To get started today, please call our office at (772) 569-3212 and we'll send you the new patient paperwork.

pdfFallback.heading: Technology isn't always easy.
pdfFallback.intro: If you'd rather skip the online form, you can complete these by hand and email them back to our care team. Please confirm you meet all three requirements below before sending.
pdfFallback.requirementsLabel: To register this way, you must be:
requirements: Covered by an insurance plan we accept
A current Vero Beach Pediatrics patient family — during our exclusive enrollment period for VBP families

priviaForms.heading: Privia Medical Group forms & notices.
priviaForms.intro: Vero Beach Adult Medicine is a member of Privia Medical Group. The notices and forms below apply to all our patients — review or download any you need. Your care team can also provide paper copies at your visit.

items.title: Notice of Privacy Practices
items.description: Describes how your health information may be used and disclosed, and how you can access your individually identifiable health information. Please review this notice carefully.

items.title: Authorization for Release of Medical Information
items.description: Allows you to authorize the disclosure of your health information to a designated individual, company, agency, or facility.

items.title: Authorization & Consent for Treatment
items.description: Your consent for treatment, communications (calls, emails, and text messaging), and agreement of financial responsibility.
items.spanish: Autorización y Consentimiento Para el Tratamiento.

items.title: Preferred Contacts
items.description: Encouraged but not required — lets us know who we may share information with on your behalf.

items.title: Financial Policy
items.description: Advises patients of their complete financial responsibility for all medical services received, without regard to insurance eligibility or coverage determinations.

items.title: Language Services
items.description: Free language assistance and communication aids, with our Notice of Nondiscrimination (English & Spanish).
